rationale: The citing paper is about a household-level joint incidence/brand-choice model with a no-purchase option, while the cited paper is about retailer category pricing decomposition and store-brand objectives at the chain level, so direct substantive overlap appears limited. However, both use scanner data and random-coefficients demand ideas, making a methodological/background use plausible. With only a DOI match and no in-text citation context, the evidence is too sparse to safely drop as obvious mention-only.
